name: Quality Tier 6 — CTE / window / running totals
description: Window functions and CTEs. Expected = row count (window-functions on per-row analytics return one row per source row; CTEs typically aggregate). Tests the LlmDirectSqlEmitter escape valve too — many of these shapes don't fit QuerySpec form-filling.

Scenarios.Question: monthly bill total over the last 24 months
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT FORMAT(IssuedAt,'yyyy-MM'), SUM(TotalAmount) GROUP BY month
Scenarios.Notes: Date-bucketing via computed expression. 24 months of data present.

Scenarios.Question: running total of bill amounts ordered by issue date
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT IssuedAt, TotalAmount, SUM(TotalAmount) OVER (ORDER BY IssuedAt) — window function, one row per bill (capped at MaxRows)
Scenarios.Notes: Running total via SUM() OVER. MaxRows cap should produce 1000.

Scenarios.Question: top 3 customers by total amount paid
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Customers.FullNameEn, SUM(Bills.TotalAmount) GROUP BY customer ORDER BY sum DESC LIMIT 3 (or via window with RANK<=3)
Scenarios.Notes: Simple top-N — should NOT need a CTE or window. Tests that ORDER BY + LIMIT goes through cleanly.

Scenarios.Question: with a cte rank departments by ticket count
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: WITH cte AS (SELECT DepartmentId, COUNT(*) FROM Tickets GROUP BY DepartmentId) SELECT * FROM cte ORDER BY count DESC — or equivalent window
Scenarios.Notes: Explicit CTE request. Tests LlmDirectSqlEmitter escape valve; 60 departments total (most have 0 tickets — the result might cap at fewer).
